metadata.title: En Chakai — Authentic Japanese Tea Ceremony Experience in Tokyo
metadata.description: Experience the art of traditional Japanese tea ceremony in Tokyo. An intimate, authentic chado experience unlike any other.

hero.subtitle: 円茶会
hero.heading:
A Quiet Moment.
A Lifetime of Memory.
hero.tagline: An intimate journey into the heart of chado — traditional Japanese tea ceremony — in Tokyo.

experience.label: Your Experience
experience.heading: What Awaits You
experience.p1: Step through the garden gate and leave the city behind. In our intimate tea room, you will experience the profound stillness of an authentic tea ceremony — not as a spectator, but as a welcomed guest.
experience.p2: From the first bow to the last sip of matcha, every moment is crafted to immerse you in the beauty of wabi-sabi. Feel the warmth of a hand-thrown tea bowl, taste seasonal wagashi made that morning, and discover the meditative rhythm of chado.
experience.p3: Whether this is your first encounter with tea ceremony or a return to a beloved practice, our host will guide you with warmth and expertise through an experience you will carry with you long after you leave.

authentic.title: Authentic Tradition
authentic.description: Urasenke-school tea ceremony practiced in a genuine chashitsu (tea room)

intimate.title: Intimate Setting
intimate.description: Small groups of 2-6 guests for a deeply personal experience

seasonal.title: Seasonal Beauty
seasonal.description: Each ceremony reflects the season through wagashi, chabana, and scroll selection

plans.label: Experience Plans
plans.heading: Choose Your Journey
plans.description: Three carefully curated experiences, each offering a different depth of engagement with the Way of Tea.

ume.name: 梅 Ume
ume.subtitle: Introduction
ume.description: A graceful introduction to the world of chado. Observe and participate in a simplified tea ceremony while enjoying seasonal wagashi.
includes: Brief explanation of tea ceremony principles

take.name: 竹 Take
take.subtitle: Immersion
take.description: A deeper engagement with the tea ceremony. Learn the fundamental movements and philosophy of chado, and prepare your own bowl of matcha.

matsu.name: 松 Matsu
matsu.subtitle: The Complete Way
matsu.description: The definitive chado experience. From the garden path to the final bow, experience every element of an authentic tea gathering as it has been practiced for centuries.
includes: Tea utensil study with historical context

bookingCta.heading: Begin Your Journey
bookingCta.description: Reserve your tea ceremony experience and step into a world of tranquility and tradition.

access.label: Access
access.heading: Finding Us
access.station: Sengoku Station (Toei Mita Line) — 5 min walk
access.description: Nestled in the quiet residential neighborhood of Bunkyo ward, our tea room offers a retreat from the energy of central Tokyo. The area's unhurried atmosphere is itself part of the experience.

directions.title: Directions
directions.step2: Walk north along Hakusan-dori for 3 minutes
directions.step3: Turn right at the small shrine
directions.step4: Our entrance is 2 minutes ahead on the left

tripadvisor.heading: Guest Reviews
tripadvisor.description: Read what our guests say about their experience.

review1.comment: The most authentic tea ceremony experience I've had in Japan. Our host was incredibly gracious and knowledgeable. Far from the tourist traps — this is the real thing.
review1.author: Sarah M. — New York, USA

review2.comment: A truly meditative experience. The attention to detail, from the seasonal wagashi to the hand-selected scroll, was extraordinary. We felt like honoured guests, not tourists.
review2.author: James T. — London, UK

review3.comment: We came for tea and left with a completely new understanding of Japanese culture. The intimate setting and patient guidance made all the difference. Highly recommend the Take plan.
review3.author: Emily R. — Melbourne, Australia

whatToPrepare.label: Before You Visit
whatToPrepare.heading: What to Prepare
whatToPrepare.description: A few simple preparations to help you make the most of your tea ceremony experience.

whiteSocks.title: White Socks
whiteSocks.reason: Guests enter the tea room in socks. White or light-colored socks are traditional and show respect for the clean tatami space.

removeAccessories.title: Remove Watches & Rings
removeAccessories.reason: Jewelry and watches can scratch the delicate tea bowls and utensils, some of which are centuries old.

avoidPerfume.title: Avoid Strong Perfume
avoidPerfume.reason: The tea ceremony engages all senses, including smell. Strong fragrances can interfere with the subtle aroma of the matcha and incense.

comfortableClothing.title: Wear Comfortable Clothing
comfortableClothing.reason: You will be seated on tatami for the duration of the ceremony. Loose, comfortable clothing makes the experience more enjoyable.

host.label: Your Host
host.heading: The Tea Master
host.name: Tea Master Yamamoto
host.philosophy: In tea, there is no competition, no hurry, no judgement — only the beauty of this single, unrepeatable moment shared between host and guest.
host.bio: Trained in the Urasenke tradition since youth, our tea master has dedicated over two decades to the study and teaching of chado. Having welcomed guests from more than 30 countries, they bring warmth, patience, and a deep reverence for the Way of Tea to every ceremony.

booking.heading: Reserve Your Experience
booking.description: Select your preferred plan and date. We will confirm your reservation within 24 hours.

fields.name: Full Name

success.heading: Reservation Received

policy.intro: We understand that plans may change. Please review our cancellation policy below.
policy.note: All cancellation requests will be processed within 2 business days. Refunds will be issued to the original payment method.

footer.tagline: The Way of Tea, Authentically Yours
